summary: sensivity ships a FiveM/GTA-V game cheat with stealth Windows autostart persistence and obfuscated eval payload
details: The package is published to npm under the description 'Sensivity Control Panel' but actually distributes a Windows game cheat for FiveM / GTA V together with covert persistence and an obfuscated runtime payload. On CLI invocation, launcher.js writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run autostart entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing at wscript.exe running a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches launcher.js — the registry name, the VBS filename, and the runtime process.title 'Runtime Broker' are deliberate impersonations of legitimate Microsoft components. launcher.js then loads server.obf.js (a 181KB obfuscator.io-packed blob using a string-array + RC4 decoder) and runs it via eval(fs.readFileSync(...)); the decoded blob enumerates GTAProcess*/FiveM* processes, references qb-core/spawnmanager/mapmanager, and exposes startCheat / stopEventLogger / performAction over socket.io. The shipped sens.node (6.8 MB Windows PE) contains cheat-internal strings ('License Accepted', 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head' aimbot bones). On the 'checkLicense' socket event, the package collects HKLM Cryptography MachineGuid (via PowerShell), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial, SHA-256 hashes them into an HWID, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL embedded in the obfuscated blob. Separately, the CLI 'update' subcommand runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe`, force-terminating every Node and PowerShell process owned by the user — destroying unrelated builds, dev servers, editor extension hosts, and admin scripts. The combination of disguised autostart persistence, eval of an obfuscated blob, hardware-fingerprint exfiltration, deceptive package metadata, and destructive process kills constitutes a hostile package any developer who installs and runs it will be compromised by.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
